title: Pattern guard warning fix
body: This is an attempt to address [#110489](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/110489) * Added `bool p_is_guard` parameter to `resolve_node`, `reduce_expression`, `resolve_suite` and `reduce_call` * From `resolve_match_branch` I pass `true` into `resolve_suite` when handling the guard_body * In reduce_call I don't raise an error if `p_is_guard=true` Example of warning being called for an unconsumed return as opposed to guard pattern: * Fixes #110489.
author: StamLord

linked_issues.title: `RETURN_VALUE_DISCARDED` warning raised when using any function call as `match` pattern guard

issue_comments.body: I think you should use the existing `p_is_root` parameter, which is specifically used for this purpose. That is, the pattern guard expression should not be treated as a root.
Ah I think you might be right. I will test it later today and update.
Thanks @dalexeev. Treating the guard_body as non-root makes much more sense.

label_notes: GDScript pattern-guard false-warning fix. StamLord (first-time contributor) addressed reviewer guidance to reuse existing p_is_root param rather than introducing a new boolean flag.
